coredata.dc:title: A novel colorimetric and fluorometric dual-signal identification of crude baijiu based on La-CDs

Xia, Xuhui
Zheng, Jia
Yuan, Zirui
Luo, Yiyao

Ma, Yi
Huo, Danqun
Hou, Changjun
coredata.dc:description: The correct classification of strong-flavored crude baijiu affects its quality and overall standard and is crucial for the intelligent development of the baijiu industry. In this work, we developed a novel optical sensing array using lanthanum-doped carbon dots (La-CDs). Using La-CDs with three metal complex dyes—chromium black T, alizarin red, and dimethylphenol orange—we were able to detect organic acids and tannic acid (TA) in crude baijiu in a way that was both colorimetric and fluorescent for the first time. Based on the indicator displacement (IDA) principle, organic acids competitively replace the dyes' binding sites on La3+, causing the dye colors to change to varying degrees. TA quenches the fluorescence of quantum dots through an internal filtering effect. We analyzed the data using pattern recognition algorithms such as HCA, PCA, and LDA, successfully classifying and identifying 16 types of strong-flavored crude baijiu, which included 10 types of carboxylic acids and various grades. In blind tests of 32 crude baijiu samples, the colorimetric method achieved a 94 % accuracy rate, while the fluorescence method achieved 100 %. The sensor demonstrates significant advantages in response speed.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Crude baijiu
Quantum dots
Tannin
Colorimetric sensor array
Fluorescent sensor array

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Preparation and optimization of EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, and XO-CDs@La probes
TA and organic acid identification experiments
Classification and identification of crude baijiu

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (NO. 31171684 ), Sichuan Science and Technology Program ( 21ZYZFZDYF0019 ), Strong-flavor Baijiu Solid-state Fermentation Key Laboratory of China light industry ( 2022JJ001 )\uFF0C Wuliangye Group research project ( CXY2020ZR004 )\uFF0C Chongqing Graduate Tutor Team Construction Project, Analytical and Testing Center of Chongqing University (the characterization of TEM/XPS/FT-IR ) and the sharing fund of Chongqing University's large equipment.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) TEM image of La-CDs; (b) FT-IR plot of La-CDs、EBT-CDs@La、AR-CDs@La、XO-CDs@La;XPS analysis of La-CDs (c); (d) C1s; (e) N1s; (f) O1s; (g) La3d; (h) fluorescence lifetime of La-CDs with and without TA.

Colorimetric sensing array feasibility study EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, XO-CDs@La UV spectrograms: (a), (b), (c). Fluorescence sensing array feasibility study EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, XO-CDs@La fluorescence spectrograms: (d), (e), (f).
EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, XO-CDs@La pH optimization (a), (b), (c). optimization of quantum dots and dye dosage for EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, XO-CDs@La (d), (e), (f).
Colorimetric identification results of 9 organic acids and TAnic acids. (a) Fingerprints. (b) Heat maps. (c) PCA. (d)HCA. (e)LDA.
Colorimetric identification results of 16 strong-flavored crude baijiu. (a) Fingerprints. (b) Heat maps. (c) PCA. (d)HCA. (e)LDA.
Schematic illustration of the fluorometric and colorimetric sensor array for the identification of organics acids and crude baijiu on La-CDs-dyes.
Comparison of the presented study with some published methods.

head.ce:title: A novel colorimetric and fluorometric dual-signal identification of crude baijiu based on La-CDs

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory for Biorheological Science and Technology of Ministry of Education

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The correct classification of strong-flavored crude baijiu affects its quality and overall standard and is crucial for the intelligent development of the baijiu industry. In this work, we developed a novel optical sensing array using lanthanum-doped carbon dots (La-CDs). Using La-CDs with three metal complex dyes—chromium black T, alizarin red, and dimethylphenol orange—we were able to detect organic acids and tannic acid (TA) in crude baijiu in a way that was both colorimetric and fluorescent for the first time. Based on the indicator displacement (IDA) principle, organic acids competitively replace the dyes' binding sites on La3+, causing the dye colors to change to varying degrees. TA quenches the fluorescence of quantum dots through an internal filtering effect. We analyzed the data using pattern recognition algorithms such as HCA, PCA, and LDA, successfully classifying and identifying 16 types of strong-flavored crude baijiu, which included 10 types of carboxylic acids and various grades. In blind tests of 32 crude baijiu samples, the colorimetric method achieved a 94 % accuracy rate, while the fluorescence method achieved 100 %. The sensor demonstrates significant advantages in response speed.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Baijiu has a lengthy history and cultural legacy, having been developed and innovated over thousands of years to create a rich and colorful flavor features (L. Qiao et al., 2023; Wang et al., 2021). Nowadays, baijiu has become one of the six major distilled spirits in the world. China is the country with the largest production and consumption of distilled spirits in the world, and the baijiu industry has also become one of the important industries of the national economy. (Dong et al., 2024). We classify baijiu into 12 categories based on variations in the main flavor compounds. These categories include strong, clear, rice, and soy sauce, among others. The strong-flavored baijiu, also known as Luzhou-flavor baijiu, is characterized by the presence of ethyl caproate as the primary aroma compound (Du et al., 2021; Zheng & Han, 2016). Due to its pleasant fragrance, gentle nature, and sweet qualities, customers have a significant preference for it, resulting in a market dominance of 70 % for strong-flavored baijiu. Crude baijiu is an intermediate product derived from grains that undergo boiling, saccharification, fermentation, and distillation. It is not yet stored or blended, and serves as the precursor to baijiu (Hu et al., 2021; Jin et al., 2017). Compared with finished baijiu, crude baijiu also has more impurities, a more pungent taste and a stronger sense of stimulation. The accurate judgment and classification of the quality level of the crude baijiu determines the quality of the crude baijiu and the mixing effect of the finished baijiu, and it is a very important link in the production of baijiu to control the quality of the product.

Luzhou-flavor baijiu are rich in trace components, mainly including alcohols, esters, acids, aldehydes and ketones, aromatics, and other trace components (Wang, Jing, et al., 2022). Various trace components affect the flavor and taste of baijiu, and the differences in the content and types of trace components form the different stylistic characteristics of baijiu. The types and contents of flavor substances are different in cellars of different ages, and the older the cellar, the more microorganisms there are, which can produce richer flavor substances (Hu et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2022). The quality of crude baijiu distilled from the same cellar tends to be higher the closer it is to the bottom of the cellar. There are also large differences in the crude baijiu distilled at different time periods, with the first period generally containing a large amount of acetaldehyde and other low-boiling point substances, which are of poor quality and used for redistillation (He et al., 2022); The second period of ethyl caproate, ethyl acetate, and volatile flavor compounds increases, and the aroma of the crude baijiu is strong; it is commonly used as a flavored baijiu (Li et al., 2019); The third stage baijiu with higher output has lower aldehydes, increased ethyl lactate and acetic acid concentration, and inferior aroma harmonization, and is commonly utilized for finished baijiu blending (Chen et al., 2024). The fourth stage baijiu has higher acids, aldehydes, and esters, with a bitter flavor (H. Liu & Sun, 2018);and the fifth stage, where the alcohol level drops to twenty degrees, contains more ethyl oleate and other higher fatty acids, and the spirit begins to become cloudy, is generally used for secondary distillation (Tian et al., 2023). The above factors make it challenging to classify crude baijiu, which typically requires professional sommeliers to taste, classify into one, two, three, and four grades, encapsulate different grades into different ceramic altars for storage, and further determine through physical and chemical index tests (Ye et al., 2022). Currently, the classification of crude baijiu mainly relies on the testing of physical and chemical indexes and sensory evaluation. Sensory evaluation is susceptible to subjective factors, and there is no uniform standard. Long-term tasting will inevitably lead to sensory fatigue in the tasters, a process that is time-consuming, laborious, and not feasible in batches. Therefore, it is often necessary to use liquid chromatography (X. Zhang et al., 2020), gas chromatography (Zhao et al., 2018), liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometry (Sun et al., 2018), gas chromatography tandem mass spectrometry (Han et al., 2017), and other large-scale instruments for assistance However, these instruments have higher analytical conditions, complex pre-treatment, and longer analysis times, which limit their application in the industrialized and intelligent production of baijiu.

Indicator displacement assay (IDA) is a unique and innovative molecular sensing method that has emerged in recent years. Indicators can reversibly bind to receptors through intermolecular interactions (e.g., hydrogen bonding, electrostatic interactions, ligand bonding, π-π stacking, hydrophobic interactions, etc.) to form supramolecular assemblies (Ma et al., 2009; Välimäki et al., 2018) Displacement of the indicator from the host under competition after the addition of a competing analyte results in a unique optical response if the binding affinity of the indicator to the receptor is less than the binding affinity of an analyte with the same receptor. The IDA method has a number of advantages over conventional sensing techniques. First, the assay relies on non-covalent/supramolecular bonds to bind the indicator and receptor, eliminating the need for complex synthesis reactions. Secondly, these supramolecular bonds utilize the same receptor, allowing for the tuning of the output signal by varying the different indicators, and facilitating the rapid adaptation of the technique to different receptors for the analysis of the desired analyte. The simplicity, low cost, high sensitivity, and selectivity of IDA have led to its rapid development in the field of sensing array detection. Researchers have currently used IDA-based colorimetric sensors to determine Chinese tea varieties (M. Jia et al., 2021), total acids during apple cider vinegar fermentation (Wang, Xiong, et al., 2022), and anions. (Feng et al., 2012).

Carbon dots (CDs), which have excellent luminescent properties, low toxicity, and simple synthesis, have become increasingly popular in fluorescent probes, bioimaging, and biochemical sensing in recent years (Gui et al., 2017; Z. Xu et al., 2022; Zhu et al., 2019). Because of their great performance, several CDs have been used to build sensors for the quick detection of Baijiu. For example, Yao et al. designed a three-channel colorimetric sensing array based on triple color-emitting quantum dots for the rapid detection of base wines (Fan et al., 2020); Zhang et al. screened acid-sensitive CDs and designed a triple fluorescence-emitting CDs sensor based on the electrostatic interactions between the CDs and organic acids for the classification and identification of Baijiu (Zhang et al., 2023).

However, in many applications, the sensors are based on a single colorimetric or fluorescent signal. Colorimetric sensing technologies offer tremendous potential in even though detection (POCT) due to their cost-effectiveness, simplicity, and naked eye signal readout, but they always have the problem of low detection sensitivity. Fluorescence sensing, on the other hand, is poor due to the strong biological background and lack of spatial resolution. Dual-signal sensing technology can compensate for the inherent limits of both sensors while combining their distinct advantages. CDs have a high concentration of carboxyl (-COOH), hydroxyl, and amino groups on their surfaces, and lanthanide metal treatment improves their fluorescence response, Stoke shift, and fluorescence lifespan (M. Xu et al., 2019). Metal complex dyes compound with metal ions in a certain pH range and undergo significant color changes, therefore combining the two to create colorimetric and fluorescent dual-signal probes can improve the accuracy and reliability of the result output by leveraging the benefits of each. Data analysis through machine learning algorithms for pattern recognition. The primary methods used in pattern recognition analysis are Principal Component Analysis (PCA), Hierarchical Data Cluster Analysis (HCA), and Linear Discriminant (LDA). PCA identifies orthogonal correlations among principal components by utilizing data characteristics for dimensionality reduction, which helps to some extent in reducing background noise. HCA constructs similarity clustering trees by measuring and calculating the similarities among different classes of datasets using unsupervised learning techniques. LDA conducts a difference measure that accurately reflects the distinctions between samples.

In this study (See Scheme 1 ), we constructed a colorimetric and fluorescent dual-signal sensing array for the identification of crude baijiu using La-CDs and three metal complex dyes, based on indicator displacement (IDA) and inner filter effect (IFE). The binding of La-CDs to chromium black T (EBT), alizarin red (AR), and dimethylphenol orange (XO), respectively, induces dramatic color changes, and the organic acids in the crude baijiu contribute to these color changes. Small molecules compete with dyes for binding to metal ions, resulting in a distinct colorimetric signal. On the other hand, the presence of TA in the crude baijiu leads to the quenching of La-CDs fluorescence, which in turn triggers a fluorescent signal response. We identified 16 crude baijiu and 10 organic acids using the machine learning techniques PCA, HCA, and LDA.

Formic acid, butyric acid, lactic acid, caproic acid, malonic acid, tannic acid (TA), tartaric acid, terephthalic acid, trimesinic acid, 2,6-pyridinedicarboxylic acid (DPA), ascorbic acid, benzaldehyde, ethyl caproate, ethyl lactate, boric acid, sodium borate, formamide, lanthanum nitrate hexahydrate, reduced glutathione, alizarin red, chromium black T, and xylenol orange were purchased from Aladdin. The strong-flavored crude baijiu used in this study were purchased from Luzhou Laojiao, details of which are shown in Table S1 and S2. All aqueous solutions use deionized water from the Millipore Direct-Q Water system (Mosheim, France) with a resistivity of 18.2 MΩ cm−1. It is critical to emphasize that all of the reagents employed in this investigation are chemically pure and applied directly. The main experimental equipment is shown in Table S3.

We synthesized La-CDs rapidly using a one-pot process. Ultrasonicate 4.16 mL of ammonium formamide, 30 mg of lanthanum nitrate hexahydrate, and 330 mg of reduced glutathione for 10 min. Transfer to a 30 mL reactor and heat at 180 °C for 60 min. After cooling to ambient temperature, we centrifuged the supernatant at 10,000 r for 10 min to remove insoluble particles. It was then filtered through a 0.2 mm filter membrane and frozen in the refrigerator at 4 °C.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation and optimization of EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, and XO-CDs@La probes
ce:section.ce:para: Preparation of quantum dots and dye probes. To begin, we put prepared La-CDs, boric acid buffer, and metal complex dyes EBT, AR, and XO (10 mM) into centrifuge tubes. This made the EBT-CDs@La probes, the AR-CDs@La probes, and the XO-CDs@La probes. We explored the buffer pH and the ratios of quantum dots to dyes for the three array sites through one-way experiments to enhance the sensitivity of the sensing arrays. (1) To optimize the buffer pH, we prepared a boric acid buffer with different pH levels, added 100 mL of G4 crude baijiu samples to the array points, stirred well, and obtained the UV spectra using a UV spectrophotometer. We then extracted and processed the UV absorbance of EBT (A670/A537), AR (A528/A448), and XO (A579/A450) for analysis, respectively. (2) Find the best ratios of quantum dots and dyes by keeping the amounts of BET, AR, and XO at 100 mL, 100 mL, and 50 mL, respectively. Then, change the amount of quantum dots that are added and use buffer to make up the difference. This will keep the total volume of the system the same. We added 100 μL of G4 crude baijiu samples and mixed them well. Then, we used a UV spectrophotometer to get the spectral data of EBT (A670/A537), AR (A528/A448), and XO (A579/A450), and we looked at the results in more detail.

ce:section.ce:section-title: TA and organic acid identification experiments
ce:section.ce:para:
Based on the optimized EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, and XO-CDs@La sensing array points. We selected nine small molecules of different organic acids, tannins (10 mM), from the colorimetric sensing array to add to the reaction system. We obtained the UV spectra using a UV spectrophotometer, extracted the UV absorbance of EBT (670/537), AR (528/448), and XO (579/450), and categorized and identified the small molecules using the machine learning algorithms PCA, HCA, and LDA. In order to further study the quantitative analysis ability of colorimetric sensors for organic acids, a series of DPA with different concentrations were tested. The ratio of ultraviolet absorption peaks EBT (670/537), AR (528/448) and XO (579/450) at the three array points before and after the reaction were expressed by M1, M2 and M3, respectively. The Euclidean distance EDM was calculated by the formula (2-1), and linear regression analysis was performed for the concentration of organic acids.
(2-1)
EDM =
M
1 2
+ M
2 2
+ M
3 2

In the fluorescence sensing array, we selected eight small molecules, including acids, esters, aldehydes, and antioxidants. The fluorescent array's selectivity was assessed by analyzing fluorescence spectra at 450 nm in response to excitation at 395 nm. The fluorescent array's response was used to quantitatively detect TA. The ratio of fluorescence absorption peaks EBT ((I0-I1)/I0), AR ((I0-I1)/I0) and XO ((I0-I1)/I0) of the three array points at 450 nm before and after the reaction were expressed by F1, F2 and F3, respectively. The Euclidean distance EDF was calculated by the formula (2-2) and linear regression analysis was performed for the concentration of tannic acid.
(2-2)
EDF =
F
1 2
+ F
2 2
+ F
3 2

ce:section.ce:section-title: Classification and identification of crude baijiu
ce:section.ce:para: In order to deeply investigate the sensing array's practical application capacity, we collected samples of various grades of crude baijiu made in four different cellars, totaling 16 crude baijiu samples. Raw data was collected using colorimetric and fluorescence detection methods, with each sample repeated three times to create a 16 × 3 × 3 matrix. Crude baijiu samples are classified and identified using PCA, HCA, and LDA algorithms.

The morphology and microstructure of La-CDs were characterized by TEM images. As shown in Fig. 1a, the La-CDs were well dispersed with regular shapes and diameters of about 7–12 nm. The functional groups on the surfaces of La-CDs, EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, and XO-CDs@La were investigated using Fourier infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR), as shown in Fig. 1b, the broad peaks observed between 3650 and 3120 cm−1 are attributed to the stretching vibrations of OH and NH groups. These functional hydrophilic groups on the surface significantly enhance the water solubility of La-CDs. Additionally, typical peaks at 1651.86 cm−1 and 1297.42 cm−1 are caused by the stretching vibrations of CO and CN bonds, while the peaks at 1574.77 cm−1 and 1379.34 cm−1 are due to the stretching vibrations of CN and CO. The peak observed at 1003.10 cm−1 originates from the stretching vibration of CS (Zhou et al., 2021).The peaks between 1651.86 and 1379.34 cm−1 and those between 3650 and 3120 cm−1 in EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, and XO-CDs@La show a reduction or disappearance, indicating that La forms complexes with the dyes through coordination involving carbonyl and hydroxyl groups (Mohamed et al., 2015).

The functional groups and elemental analysis of La-CDs were analyzed using X-ray electron spectroscopy, as shown in Fig. 1c, four elements, C, N, O, and La, are present in La-CDs, with 284.8, 400.3, and 532.2 eV attributed to C1s, N1s, and O1s, while the characteristic peaks at 838.9 and 852.2 eV are attributed to La3d. where the high-resolution energy spectrum of C1s (Fig. 1d) showed three characteristic peaks at 284.8, 288.3, and 285.93 eV matched to C-C/C=C, C=O/C=N, and N-C=O chemical bonds, respectively (Gao et al., 2021). 398.9, 399.6, and 402.3 eV in N1s were attributed to Pyridinic N, Pyrrolic N, and Graphitic N (Fig. 1e) (Li et al., 2021). 529.9 and 531.6 eV in O1s were attributed to CO and C-OH/C-O-C, respectively (Fig. 1f) (Wang, Su, et al., 2022). The results indicate that La-CDs contain a large amount of COO-, -OH and NH2- consistent with the FT-IR results.

To explore the arrays' sensing mechanism, the UV spectral changes of quantum dots, dyes, and quantum dots, dyes, quantum dots, and carboxylic acid compounds were studied (Fig. 2a). The EBT showed blue color in boric acid rinse solution at pH 7.8, with the characteristic absorption peaks at 625 and 670 nm. When La-CDs were introduced, the solution quickly changed from blue to purple-red. The absorption peaks at 625 nm were blue-shifted. When La-CDs were added, the solution quickly changed from blue to purple-red, the absorption peak at 625 nm was blue-shifted, and a new absorption peak emerged at 537 nm. When DPA was added, the solution instantly turned blue and the absorption peaks returned to 625 and 670 nm, indicating that DPA had displaced the ligand from the EBT-CDs@La complex (Yilmaz & Oktem, 2018). Fig. 2(b) AR shows a yellow color in a boric acid solution at pH 6.8 with a characteristic absorption peak at 448 nm; immediately after the addition of La-CDs, the solution changes to a peach color with the UV peak red shifted to 528 nm; when DPA is added, the solution returns to yellow and the absorption peak at 448 nm increases. In Fig. 2(c), XO in pH 6.0 buffer showed brownish yellow color with characteristic absorption peaks at 450 and 579 nm, and the solution of quantum dots showed orange-red color, and the absorbance decreased at 450 nm, but the absorbance increased significantly at 579 nm, which may be due to the coordination of ligands and metal ions extending the electronic domains in the ligands' aromatic functional groups (Cao et al., 2023) The solution turned yellow quickly following the addition of DPA, the absorption peak at 450 nm restored, and the absorption peak at 579 nm reduced dramatically as compared to XO. The results demonstrated that the addition of quantum dots to the three dyes resulted in a complex formation between the positively charged La3+ and the metal complex dyes, causing a specific color response and accompanying absorption peak migration. Upon adding the carboxylic acid compounds, their stronger competitive binding with La3+ caused them to replace the indicator, reducing the color shift. Additionally, each carboxylic acid compound's competitive binding ability varied, resulting in distinct fingerprints. Each carboxylic acid compound has a distinct competitive binding ability, resulting in a unique fingerprint. The naked eye is capable of detecting significant color changes, making it suitable for colorimetric sensing of carboxylic acid compounds. Crude baijiu has a huge number of organic acids, and different grades of baijiu have varied types and amounts of carboxylic acid compounds, resulting in the differentiation of crude baijiu.

To further understand the fluorescence sensing process of the sensing arrays, we examined the fluorescence spectra of quantum dots, quantum dots and dyes, quantum dots, dyes and carboxylic acid compounds, and quantum dots, dyes, and TA (Figs. 2d-f). The N-CDs exhibit blue-green fluorescence at 450 nm when excited at 395 nm, and the fluorescence intensity decreases significantly upon complex formation with dyes, possibly due to the adsorption of anthraquinone dyes on the surface of La-CDs through electrostatic interaction. The photoluminescence strength of La-CDs is reduced by dye absorption through complex formation or energy transfer (Chettri et al., 2019). The fluorescence colors of EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La and XO-CDs@La were altered. Upon addition of DPA, the fluorescence intensity slightly recovered due to the strong binding ability of DPA, which disrupted the complex probe and released formed DPA-La from chromium metal compound dye into buffer solution (M. Xu et al., 2019). The fluorescence of the system visible to the naked eye dropped considerably with the addition of TA, which could be attributed to the fluorescence internal filtering effect (IFE) quenching quantum dot fluorescence again (Liu et al., 2019).

Quenching mechanisms can be categorized into static quenching, dynamic quenching, resonance energy transfer (FRET), photogenerated electron transfer (PET), surface energy transfer (SET), dexterous energy transfer (DET), and internal filtering effect (IFE) (Zu et al., 2017). IFE refers to the phenomenon where the absorption spectrum of an absorbent overlaps with the excitation and/or emission spectra of a fluorophore, leading to reduced fluorescence(The Huy et al., 2022). IFE is sometimes referred to as apparent quenching; however, it is not a true quenching process but rather a result of the attenuation of the excitation beam or the absorption of emitted radiation by excessive concentrations of CDs or quenchers in the solution(Zu et al., 2017). Moreover, the absorption spectrum of the fluorophore remains unchanged in the presence of the absorbent. As shown in Figs. 2 (d-f), the addition of tannic acid (1 mM) leads to a significant decrease in fluorescence at 450 nm, with the average fluorescence lifetime slightly decreasing from 2.26 ns to 1.66 ns. Fig. S1a illustrates a strong UV absorption peak for tannic acid at 375 nm, which coincides with the excitation peak of La-CDs. Additionally, we observed that the position of the fluorescence peak did not change after the addition of TA (Fig. S1b), indicating that no new substances were generated. These results further confirm that IFE may be responsible for the fluorescence quenching caused by TA. TA in sorghum is a plant secondary metabolite found primarily in the epidermis of sorghum that inhibits harmful microbes during fermentation and improves the aroma of baijiu throughout the winemaking process. Different grades of crude baijiu have different TA contents, which create different fluorescence quenching effects, resulting in the differentiation of crude baijiu.

To achieve optimal reaction conditions, we conducted one-way experiments on the pH, dye, and quantum dot ratio of the boric acid buffer to optimize the colorimetric sensing array. When the pH of the EBT-CDs@La array site was changed from 7.4 to 7.8, the UV peaks at 537 nm changed (Fig. 3 and S2) but then stayed the same. Therefore, we determined pH = 7.8 to be the ideal pH for the EBT array site. The AR-CDs@La array site's pH went up, and the absorption peak moved from 537 nm to 670 nm. This happened quickly at pH = 6.8 (Fig. 3b). The color of the solution began to shift to yellow when the pH was greater than 7.0. Consequently, we selected pH = 6.8 as the optimal pH for the AR array site in order to maintain a larger color difference before and after the addition of the target. XO-CDs@La array site The optimal pH for the XO-CDs@La array point with the largest displacement of the absorption peak (Fig. 3c) was determined to be pH = 6.0. The ratio of metal ions to dyes directly influences the array point color, sensitivity, and detection range of the target, thus we set the volume and concentration of dyes in the entire array of points after calculating the overall volume of the system through pre-experiments. The quantities of BET, AR, and XO were 100 mL, 100 mL, and 50 mL, respectively, and the concentration was 10 nm. The volume of La-CDs (0.24 mg·mL−1) was gradually adjusted, and UV spectra were taken for examination (Figs. d-f). As shown in Figs. (3 d-f), the optimal volumes of La-CDs for BET, AR, and XO were 60, 100, and 60 mL, respectively, and the exact optimized sensing array parameters are presented in Table S4.

To test the array's ability to detect carboxylic acids, we chose ten carboxylic acid molecules (10 mM), including monocarboxylic acids formic acid, butyric acid, lactic acid, and hexanoic acid, dicarboxylic acids tartaric acid and malonic acid, benzene-ringed carboxylic acids terephthalic acid, tricarboxylic acid, and TA and DPA. In a sensing array, we measured the UV profiles of 10 carboxylic acids. Each organic acid exhibited a distinct color and UV spectrum (Fig. S3). The fingerprints (Fig. 4a) and thermograms (Fig. 4b) of the data indicate that monocarboxylic acids and dyes are in competition for La3+ ability. Dibasic carboxylic acids are slightly stronger than monocarboxylic acids, and carboxylic acids with a benzene ring are more capable of binding metal ions. Pyridine dicarboxylic acid is the strongest of the three, likely due to the fact that the pyridine ring of DPA contains two carboxylic acid groups, and the nitrogen atoms of the pyridine are more susceptible to chelating with metal ions. (K. Tian et al., 2017). We evaluated the results using PCA, HCA, and LDA, as shown in Figs. 4c-d. Findings show that the sensor array can tell the difference between carboxylic compounds in a certain concentration range, correctly classifying ten organic acids in both PCA and LDA with a success rate of 100 %. Principal Component 1 (PCA1) accounts for 84.34 % of the effective information for evaluating the sensor array's performance, while Principal Component 2 (PCA2) contributes 11.90 %. By extracting these two principal components through PCA, we achieve a total effective information rate of 96.24 %, which is then used to create a two-dimensional scatter plot (Fig. 4c). In the scatter plot, organic acids from the same parallel samples cluster closely together in the same region, with each sample forming a compact cluster and clear boundaries. In the LDA analysis, multiple discriminant functions are employed to describe the differences among the various organic acids. Using the scores from the first and second linear discriminant functions as the X and Y axes, we generated a two-dimensional scatter plot (Fig. 4e), where each sample forms distinct clusters separate from others, resulting in correct classifications for all samples. In the HCA dendrogram, we measured the similarity between samples using squared Euclidean distance, merging samples based on their similarity. As depicted in Fig. 4d, the initial three parallel samples cluster into subclasses, with each type of organic acid accurately grouped. Weak competitors for metal ion binding, such as malonic acid and monocarboxylic acids, were classified together, while stronger competitors like aromatic carboxylic acids, tannins, and pyridine dicarboxylic acid formed a separate group.

We chose pyridine dicarboxylic acid, which responded better to the array, for quantitative study (Fig. S4) to further assess the sensor's performance. Linear regression analysis was carried out using Euclidean distance (EDM) and organic acid content. A linear connection was found between the log concentration of DPA in the 10–10,000 μM range. The equation was Y = -0.9814× + 2.2182 (R2 = 0.9964), with a limit of detection (LOD) of 10 μM.

La-CDs not only play a vital role in colorimetric sensor arrays, but they also have remarkable fluorescence qualities. We explored the reactions of acids, esters, aldehydes, and antioxidants to fluorescent arrays. Fig. 5 shows the results. Acids did not significantly respond to the sensing array, and there was no obvious fluorescence recovery. This might be because, to make the system more complete, we used the results of the colorimetric array optimization as the optimization conditions for the fluorescence array fixation. The dye had a stronger fluorescence quenching effect on the quantum dots, which led to a small recovery of fluorescence. After that, we proceeded with the study of esters, aldehydes, and antioxidant substances, but the degree of fluorescent change remained unchanged. The addition of TA to the fluorescence array clearly demonstrated the quantum dots' fluorescence quenching effect, which was visible to the naked eye under the UV lamp. It shows that the fluorescence sensing mechanism is susceptible to TA responses.

We further investigated the sensing arrays' ability to quantitatively detect tannins within the range of 0.01 mM–100 mM in the fluorescence spectra of each array point, observing a gradual decrease in fluorescence intensity as the tannin concentration increased. By measuring the EDF for the change in fluorescence intensity before and after, tannins were found to be linearly associated at 0.1–15 mM and 15–100 mM, respectively (Figs. S5; b-d). The linear equations were: Y = 0.0429× + 0.2716 (R2 = 0.9945) and Y = 0.0537× + 0.8444 (R2 = 0.9923), respectively. The detection limits were 0.1 and 15 mM, respectively.

Baijiu contains a variety of flavor substances, some of which can reach over 1800 kinds. These include organic acids, aldehydes, esters, alcohols, ketones, and aromatic hydrocarbons, among others. These substances give baijiu a unique aroma and taste, which determines the style characteristics. Organic acids play a significant role in influencing the flavor composition of baijiu, with significant variations in both composition and concentration across different varieties. Based on this, we analyzed 16 types of crude baijiu from four cellars using a sensing array. As shown in Fig. S6, the UV absorption peaks of the array points changed significantly after the addition of the baijiu samples. We created different fingerprints for each type of crude baijiu based on the organic acids and dyes' ability to compete for La3+ (Fig. 5a). Combining the heat map analysis (Fig. 5b) reveals a clear differentiation between the colors of different grades of crude baijiu from the same cellar. While the colors of the same grade from different cellars are more similar, they still exhibit discernible differences. PCA, HCA, and LDA were used to assess the ability of the colorimetric sensing array to discriminate 16 strong aromatic crude baijiu. Fig. 5(c) shows the results of the PCA analysis. The HCA (Fig. 5d) analysis correctly grouped the majority of the crude baijiu parallel samples, with the only misclassification being the classification of one of the G3 samples as an L3 sample (97.9 %). Furthermore, based on the LDA model, the classification accuracy for the 16 strong-flavored base wine samples was 100 %. The above findings demonstrate the excellent discriminatory ability of the sensed whole-column colorimetric method for strong-flavored crude baijiu.

Baijiu raw materials contain a high concentration of tannins, which are changed into specific aromatic compounds and polyphenols throughout the fermentation process, resulting in a smoother taste (Ma et al., 2024). Adding different grades of strong-flavored crude baijiu to the system reveals varying degrees of fluorescence quenching (Fig. S7), with each crude baijiu forming a unique fingerprint (Fig. S8a). We then processed the fluorescence data to generate the heat map Fig. S8b. We can see that, in comparison to colorimetric sensing, the fluorescence sensing array does not show a significant difference for crude baijiu of the same grade from different cellars; however, the difference between crude baijiu of different grades from the same cellar is greater. When combined with PCA and HCA analysis (Fig. S8c and S8d), we were able to correctly distinguish each type of crude baijiu, and the accuracy of the three parallel samples was 100 %. The LDA results also showed that we correctly clustered parallel samples of crude baijiu from different cellars together (Fig. S8e), while we separated crude baijiu from different cellars and grades without any erroneous clustering. The above results demonstrate the excellent performance of the fluorescence method for the identification of crude baijiu.

We subsequently tested the sensor colorimetric and fluorescence detection methods on 32 unknown strong-flavored crude baijiu samples, and the findings are presented in Fig. S9a and S9b. There was one example of recognition error in the colorimetric sensor array, which was located far from the center of the sample, while the remaining samples were successfully identified with a 94 % accuracy rate. And the fluorescence sensor array identified 32 unknown materials with a 100 % accuracy rate. The foregoing results show that both the colorimetric and fluorescent sensor arrays can identify unknown strong-flavored crude baijiu to a sufficient degree.

Table 1 presents a comparison between this study and previous research. We conducted a comprehensive evaluation of the number of fluorescent probes, array points, samples, and signal output methods across different approaches. Clearly, our sensor array requires the fewest sensing elements and array points, allowing for the identification of 16 different grades of crude baijiu from a single brand using both fluorescence and colorimetric methods. Importantly, it requires almost no reaction time, enabling detection to begin immediately after sample addition. This not only saves resources and time but also streamlines the operational process and data handling. The results demonstrate that this cost-effective, rapid-response, and user-friendly sensor array is successful in detecting crude baijiu.

We assessed the stability of the sensor arrays. This system was used to analyze crude baijiu samples, and three replicate experiments were carried out for each array point, utilizing colorimetric and fluorescence methods to determine the array points' absorbance and fluorescence intensity, respectively. The acquired data were then analyzed. Fig. S10a shows the colorimetric sensor array's detection findings, with RSDs of 0.17 %, 0.27 %, and 0.03 % for array points AR, EBT, and OX, respectively. Fig. S10b shows fluorescence sensing array detection findings with RSDs of 4.06 %, 2.1 %, and 1.4 % at AR, EBT, and OX, with RSDs <5 %. In conclusion, both fluorescence and colorimetric sensing arrays showed excellent stability.

In this study, we successfully synthesized La-CDs with desirable qualities and used them for qualitative and quantitative detection of organic acid molecules and tannins, as well as colorimetric and fluorescent dual-signal detection of crude baijiu. The prepared La-CDs exhibit high water solubility and stability. Based on these three metal complex dyes, EBT, AR, and XO, sensing probes EBT-CDs@La, AR-CDs@La, and XO-CDs@La were developed for the identification of crude baijiu, and colorimetric and fluorescent dual-signal sensing of organic acids and TA was realized using ligand substitution and the IFE effect. When combined with machine learning approaches such as LDA, PCA, and HCA, 10 distinct organic acids can be accurately identified. In the quantitative detection of DPA, ED correlated logarithmically and linearly with LgCDPA in the range of 10–10,000 μM, with a linear equation of Y = -0.98142× + 2.2182 (R2 = 0.9964). The fluorescence sensing array detected TA across a wide range, with a linear equation of Y = 0.0429× + 0.2716 (R2 = 0.9945) for low TA concentrations (0.5–15 mM) and Y = 0.0537× + 0.8444 (R2 = 0.9923) for high TA concentrations (15–100 mM). Colorimetric and fluorescence sensors were utilized to successfully identify various grades of crude baijiu from four cellars. On 32 blind samples of various crude baijiu grades, Leave-One-Out (LOO) validation yielded identification accuracies of 94 % and 100 %, respectively. In conclusion, the constructed sensor is fast, sensitive, reliable and durable, and the method provides a new idea for the identification of base wine of strong-flavored baijiu, and in the future, it can be used to realize the intelligent detection of base wine of baijiu by combining with the intelligent detection program, which shows a broad application prospect for the automation of baijiu production.

Peng Zhao: Writing – review & editing, Writing – original draft, Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Xuhui Xia: Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Jia Zheng: Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Zirui Yuan: Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Yiyao Luo: Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Huibo Luo: Project administration, Methodology, Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Yi Ma: Project administration, Methodology, Formal analysis, Data curation, Conceptualization. Danqun Huo: Resources, Project administration, Funding acquisition, Data curation, Conceptualization. Changjun Hou: Writing – review & editing, Resources, Methodology, Funding acquisition, Conceptualization.
